title.raw: 卷九 孝獻帝紀 Volume 9: Annals of Emperor Xiaoxian

sentences.zh: 九月甲戌，即皇帝位，年九歲。

translations.literal: On jiaxu of the ninth month, he assumed the imperial seat, aged nine.
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu in the ninth month the boy emperor took the throne; he was nine years old.

sentences.zh: 遷皇太后於永安宮。

translations.literal: He moved the empress dowager to Yongan Palace.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager was relocated to Yongan Palace.

sentences.zh: 大赦天下。

translations.literal: He issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty was proclaimed throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 改昭寧為永漢。

translations.literal: He changed Zhaoning to Yonghan.
translations.idiomatic: The era name was changed from Zhaoning to Yonghan.

sentences.zh: 丙子，董卓殺皇太后何氏。

translations.literal: On bingzi, Dong Zhuo killed Empress Dowager née He.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi Dong Zhuo had Empress Dowager He murdered.

sentences.zh: 初令侍中、給事黃門侍郎員各六人。

translations.literal: For the first time he fixed the palace attendants and the gentlemen consultants at the Yellow Gates at six men each.
translations.idiomatic: The court first fixed the establishment at six palace attendants and six gentlemen consultants at the yellow gates.

sentences.zh: 賜公卿以下至黃門侍郎家一人為郎，以補宦官所領諸署，侍於殿上。

translations.literal: He bestowed on the households of the ministers of state down to the gentlemen consultants at the Yellow Gates one man each as gentleman, to fill the various offices the eunuchs had led, serving in the hall.
translations.idiomatic: One son from each household from the highest ministers down to the gentlemen consultants at the yellow gates was appointed gentleman to staff the bureaus the eunuchs had controlled, serving in the palace halls.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，以太尉劉虞為大司馬。

translations.literal: On yiyou, Grand Commandant Liu Yu was made grand marshal.
translations.idiomatic: On yiyou Liu Yu, grand commandant, was appointed grand marshal.

sentences.zh: 董卓自為太尉，加鈇鉞、虎賁。

translations.literal: Dong Zhuo personally became Grand Commandant, with added axe and halberd insignia and tiger lancers.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo took the grand commandant’s post for himself, with the ceremonial axe and halberd and a tiger-lancer guard.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，太中大夫楊彪為司空。

translations.literal: On bingxu, Grand Counselor Yang Biao was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu Yang Biao, grand counselor, became minister of works.

sentences.zh: 甲午，豫州牧黃琬為司徒。

translations.literal: On jiawu, Shepherd of Yu Huang Wan was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu Huang Wan, shepherd of Yu province, was appointed minister of education.

sentences.zh: 遣使弔祠故太傅陳蕃、大將軍竇武等。

translations.literal: He sent envoys to offer condolences and sacrifice to former Grand Tutor Chen Fan, General-in-Chief Dou Wu, and others.
translations.idiomatic: Envoys were dispatched to mourn and sacrifice at the shrines of Chen Fan, Dou Wu, and the other victims of the earlier purge.

sentences.zh: 冬十月乙巳，葬靈思皇后。

translations.literal: On yisi of winter, the tenth month, Empress Dowager Lingsi was buried.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi in the tenth month Empress Dowager He, posthumously titled Lingsi, was interred.

sentences.zh: 白波賊寇河東，董卓遣其將牛輔擊之。

translations.literal: The White Wave bandits raided Hedong; Dong Zhuo sent his general Niu Fu to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: The White Wave raiders struck Hedong; Dong Zhuo dispatched Niu Fu against them.

sentences.zh: 十一月癸酉，董卓自為相國。

translations.literal: On guiyou of the eleventh month, Dong Zhuo personally became minister of state.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou in the eleventh month Dong Zhuo assumed the title of minister of state.

sentences.zh: 十二月戊戌，司徒黃琬為太尉，司空楊彪為司徒，光祿勳荀爽為司空。

translations.literal: On wuxu of the twelfth month, Minister of Education Huang Wan was made Grand Commandant, Minister of Works Yang Biao was made Minister of Education, and Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Xun Shuang was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu Huang Wan became grand commandant, Yang Biao moved to minister of education, and Xun Shuang took the ministry of works.

sentences.zh: 省扶風都尉，置漢安都護。

translations.literal: He abolished the Fufeng commandant and established the Han’an protector.
translations.idiomatic: The Fufeng commandant was abolished in favor of a Han’an protector.

sentences.zh: 詔除光熹、昭寧、永漢三號，還復中平六年。

translations.literal: He ordered removal of the three reign titles Guangxi, Zhaoning, and Yonghan, restoring the sixth year of Zhongping.
translations.idiomatic: An edict abolished the three short-lived era names and reverted the calendar to the sixth year of Zhongping.

sentences.zh: 初平元年春正月，山東州郡起兵以討董卓。

translations.literal: In the first year of Chuping, spring, first month, the eastern provinces east of the mountains raised troops to campaign against Dong Zhuo.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the first Chuping year commanderies east of the Taihang rose in arms against Dong Zhuo.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On xinhai, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，董卓殺弘農王。

translations.literal: On guiyou, Dong Zhuo killed the king of Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou Dong Zhuo murdered the deposed emperor, the prince of Hongnong.

sentences.zh: 白波賊寇東郡。

translations.literal: The White Wave bandits raided Dong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: White Wave raiders pillaged Dong commandery.

sentences.zh: 二月乙亥，太尉黃琬、司徒楊彪免。

translations.literal: On yihai of the second month, Grand Commandant Huang Wan and Minister of Education Yang Biao were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai in the second month Huang Wan and Yang Biao were removed as grand commandant and minister of education.

sentences.zh: 庚辰，董卓殺城門校尉伍瓊、督軍校尉周珌。

translations.literal: On gengchen, Dong Zhuo killed Commandant of the Gates Wu Qiong and Colonel Director of the Army Zhou Bi.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen Dong Zhuo executed Wu Qiong, commandant of the gates, and Zhou Bi, colonel director of the army.

sentences.zh: 以光祿勳趙謙為太尉，太僕王允為司徒。

translations.literal: Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Zhao Qian was made Grand Commandant, and Grand Coachman Wang Yun was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Qian, superintendent of the household for imperial proceedings, became grand commandant; Wang Yun, grand coachman, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 丁亥，遷都長安。

translations.literal: On dinghai, he moved the capital to Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai the court ordered the capital transferred to Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 董卓驅徙京師百姓悉西入關，自留屯畢圭苑。

translations.literal: Dong Zhuo drove and moved the common people of the capital all west through the passes, himself remaining encamped at Bigui Park.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo herded the entire population of Luoyang west through the passes while he stayed behind encamped at Bigui Park.

sentences.zh: 壬辰，白虹貫日。

translations.literal: On renchen, a white rainbow pierced the sun.
translations.idiomatic: On renchen a white rainbow spanned the sun.

sentences.zh: 三月乙巳，車駕入長安，幸未央宮。

translations.literal: On yisi of the third month, the imperial carriage entered Chang’an and visited Weiyang Palace.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi the imperial train reached Chang’an and the court took up residence in Weiyang Palace.

sentences.zh: 己酉，董卓焚洛陽宮廟及人家。

translations.literal: On jiyou, Dong Zhuo burned the Luoyang palaces, shrines, and commoners’ houses.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou Dong Zhuo torched the Luoyang palace complex, ancestral shrines, and countless homes.

sentences.zh: 戊午，董卓殺太傅袁隗、太僕袁基，夷其族。

translations.literal: On wuwu, Dong Zhuo killed Grand Tutor Yuan Kui and Grand Coachman Yuan Ji and exterminated their clans.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu Dong Zhuo slaughtered Yuan Kui, the grand tutor, and Yuan Ji, the grand coachman, and wiped out their kin.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，司空荀爽薨。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, Minister of Works Xun Shuang died.
translations.idiomatic: Xun Shuang, minister of works, died in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 六月辛丑，光祿大夫种拂為司空。

translations.literal: On xinchou of the sixth month, Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Chong Fu was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou Chong Fu, grandee of brilliant candidacy, was appointed minister of works.

sentences.zh: 大鴻臚韓融、少府陰脩、執金吾胡母班、將作大匠吳脩、越騎校尉王瓌安集關東，後將軍袁術、河內太守王匡各執而殺之，唯韓融獲免。

translations.literal: Grand Herald Han Rong, Junior Prefect Yin Xiu, Bearer of the Golden Mace Humu Ban, Court Architect Wu Xiu, and Colonel of the Agile Cavalry Wang Gui pacified the east of the passes; General of the Rear Yuan Shu and Administrator of Henei Wang Kuang each seized and killed them; only Han Rong was spared.
translations.idiomatic: Han Rong, Yin Xiu, Humu Ban, Wu Xiu, and Wang Gui were sent east to conciliate the warlords; Yuan Shu and Wang Kuang seized and executed them all except Han Rong.

sentences.zh: 董卓壞五銖錢，更鑄小錢。

translations.literal: Dong Zhuo destroyed the five-zhu cash and recast small coins.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo demonetized the five-zhu coin and issued debased small change.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月庚戌，鎮星、熒惑、太白合於尾。

translations.literal: On gengxu of winter, the eleventh month, Saturn, Mars, and Venus converged in the Tail asterism.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu in the eleventh month Saturn, Mars, and Venus met in the Tail constellation.

sentences.zh: 是歲，有司奏，和、安、順、桓四帝無功德，不宜稱宗，又恭懷、敬隱、恭愍三皇后並非正嫡，不合稱后，皆請除尊號。

translations.literal: That year, the responsible officials memorialized that the four emperors He, An, Shun, and Huan lacked merit and virtue and ought not to be called “zong,” and further that the three empresses Gonghuai, Jingyin, and Gongmin were not proper chief consorts and ought not to be called “hou,” all requesting removal of their honored titles.
translations.idiomatic: Officials argued that Emperors He, An, Shun, and Huan did not merit the temple epithet “founding ancestor,” and that Empresses Liang, Wang, and Fu had never been chief consorts, so their posthumous empress titles should be stripped.

sentences.zh: 制曰：『可。

translations.literal: The decree said, “It is permitted.”.
translations.idiomatic: The edict answered, “It may be done.”.

sentences.zh: 』孫堅殺荊州刺史王叡，又殺南陽太守張咨。

translations.literal: Sun Jian killed Inspector of Jing Wang Rui, and also killed Nanyang Governor Zhang Zi.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Jian then killed Wang Rui, the inspector of Jing, and Zhang Zi, the governor of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月辛丑，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, first month xinchou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou in the first month of the second year a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 二月丁丑，董卓自為太師。

translations.literal: On dingchou of the second month, Dong Zhuo personally became grand preceptor.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou in the second month Dong Zhuo took the title of grand preceptor.

sentences.zh: 袁術遣將孫堅與董卓將胡軫戰於陽人，軫軍大敗。

translations.literal: Yuan Shu sent his general Sun Jian to fight Dong Zhuo’s general Hu Zhen at Yangren; Zhen’s army was greatly defeated.
translations.idiomatic: The general Sun Jian, sent by Yuan Shu, clashed with Hu Zhen at Yangren and shattered Dong Zhuo’s army.

sentences.zh: 董卓遂發掘洛陽諸帝陵。

translations.literal: Dong Zhuo thereupon excavated the imperial tombs at Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo then had the Han imperial tombs around Luoyang opened and plundered.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，董卓入長安。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Dong Zhuo entered Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo arrived in Chang’an in the fourth month.

sentences.zh: 六月丙戌，地震。

translations.literal: On bingxu of the sixth month, there was an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: The earth shook on bingxu in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司空种拂免，光祿大夫濟南淳于嘉為司空。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Minister of Works Chong Fu was dismissed, and Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Chunyu Jia of Jinan was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Chong Fu left the ministry of works; Chunyu Jia of Jinan, grandee of brilliant candidacy, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 太尉趙謙罷，太常馬日磾為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Zhao Qian was removed, and Minister of Ceremonies Ma Midi was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Qian was dismissed as grand commandant; Ma Midi, minister of ceremonies, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 九月，蚩尤旗見于角、亢。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, the Chiyou banner appeared in Horn and Neck.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month a cometlike “Chiyou banner” appeared in the Horn and Neck asterisms.

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬戌，董卓殺衛尉張溫。

translations.literal: On renxu of winter, the tenth month, Dong Zhuo killed Commandant of the Guards Zhang Wen.
translations.idiomatic: On renxu in the tenth month Dong Zhuo executed Zhang Wen, commandant of the guards.

sentences.zh: 十一月，青州黃巾寇太山，太山太守應劭擊破之。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, the Qingzhou Yellow Turbans raided Mount Tai, and Mount Tai Governor Ying Shao attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Qingzhou Yellow Turbans invaded Taishan; Ying Shao, the governor, drove them off.

sentences.zh: 黃巾轉寇勃海，公孫瓚與戰於東光，復大破之。

translations.literal: The Yellow Turbans turned to raid Bohai; Gongsun Zan fought them at Dongguang and again greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Turbans swung into Bohai; Gongsun Zan crushed them again at Dongguang.

sentences.zh: 是歲，長沙有人死經月復活。

translations.literal: That year, in Changsha a man died and after a month revived.
translations.idiomatic: That year Changsha reported a man who had been dead a month and then sat up alive.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月丁丑，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, first month dingchou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou in the first month of the third year a general amnesty was granted.

sentences.zh: 袁術遣將孫堅攻劉表於襄陽，堅戰歿。

translations.literal: Yuan Shu sent his general Sun Jian to attack Liu Biao at Xiangyang; Jian died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shu ordered Sun Jian against Liu Biao at Xiangyang; Sun Jian fell in the fighting.

sentences.zh: 袁紹及公孫瓚戰於界橋，瓚軍大敗。

translations.literal: Yuan Shao and Gongsun Zan fought at Jie Bridge; Zan’s army was greatly defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shao and Gongsun Zan met at the Jie Bridge; the army of Gongsun Zan was routed.

sentences.zh: 夏四月辛巳，誅董卓，夷三族。

translations.literal: On xinsi of summer, the fourth month, Dong Zhuo was executed and his three clans exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi in the fourth month Dong Zhuo was put to death and his kin to the third degree wiped out.

sentences.zh: 司徒王允錄尚書事，總朝政，遣使者張种撫慰山東。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Wang Yun supervised the Masters of Writing and overall court government, and sent envoy Zhang Chong to console the east of the passes.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yun, minister of education, took charge of the secretariat and state affairs and sent Zhang Chong east to reassure the regional lords.

sentences.zh: 青州黃巾擊殺兗州刺史劉岱於東平。

translations.literal: The Qingzhou Yellow Turbans attacked and killed Inspector of Yan Liu Dai at Dongping.
translations.idiomatic: The Qingzhou Yellow Turbans slew Liu Dai, the inspector of Yan, at Dongping.

sentences.zh: 東郡太守曹操大破黃巾於壽張，降之。

translations.literal: Administrator of Dong commandery Cao Cao greatly defeated the Yellow Turbans at Shouzhang and accepted their surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao, governor of Dong commandery, shattered the Yellow Turbans at Shouzhang and took their surrender.

sentences.zh: 五月丁酉，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On dingyou of the fifth month, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou in the fifth month a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 丁未，征西將軍皇甫嵩為車騎將軍。

translations.literal: On dingwei, General Who Guards the West Huangfu Song was made General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei Huangfu Song, general who guards the west, was promoted to general of chariots and cavalry.

sentences.zh: 董卓部曲將李傕、郭汜、樊稠、張濟等反，攻京師。

translations.literal: Dong Zhuo’s division commanders Li Jue, Guo Si, Fan Chou, Zhang Ji, and others rebelled and attacked the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Li Jue, Guo Si, Fan Chou, Zhang Ji, and other officers of Dong Zhuo’s old army turned on the capital.

sentences.zh: 六月戊午，陷長安城，太常种拂、太僕魯旭、大鴻臚周奐、城門校尉崔烈、越騎校尉王頎並戰歿，吏民死者萬餘人。

translations.literal: On wuwu of the sixth month, they took Chang’an city; Minister of Ceremonies Chong Fu, Grand Coachman Lu Xu, Grand Herald Zhou Huan, Commandant of the Gates Cui Lie, and Colonel of the Agile Cavalry Wang Qi all died in battle; clerks and commoners who died were more than ten thousand.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu the rebels stormed Chang’an; Chong Fu, Lu Xu, Zhou Huan, Cui Lie, and Wang Qi died fighting, and over ten thousand soldiers and civilians perished.

sentences.zh: 李傕等並自為將軍。

translations.literal: Li Jue and the others together styled themselves generals.
translations.idiomatic: Li Jue and his confederates each proclaimed themselves general.

sentences.zh: 己未，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On jiwei, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei another general amnesty was issued.

sentences.zh: 李傕殺司隸校尉黃琬，甲子，殺司徒王允，皆滅其族。

translations.literal: Li Jue killed Metropolitan Commandant Huang Wan; on jiazi he killed Minister of Education Wang Yun, both exterminating their clans.
translations.idiomatic: Li Jue murdered Huang Wan, the metropolitan commandant; on jiazi he killed Wang Yun, the minister of education, and exterminated both men’s families.

sentences.zh: 丙子，前將軍趙謙為司徒。

translations.literal: On bingzi, former General-in-Chief Zhao Qian was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi Zhao Qian, former general-in-chief, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚子，太尉馬日磾為太傅，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: On gengzi of autumn, the seventh month, Grand Commandant Ma Midi was made Grand Tutor and supervised the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the seventh month Ma Midi, grand commandant, was named grand tutor with authority over the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 八月，遣日磾及太僕趙岐，持節慰撫天下。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, he sent Ma Midi and Grand Coachman Zhao Qi, bearing staff of office, to console and pacify all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Ma Midi and Zhao Qi, grand coachman, were sent with imperial credentials on a goodwill tour of the provinces.

sentences.zh: 車騎將軍皇甫嵩為太尉。

translations.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry Huangfu Song was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu Song, general of chariots and cavalry, was appointed grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 司徒趙謙罷。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Zhao Qian was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Qian was dismissed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 九月，李傕自為車騎將軍，郭汜後將軍，樊稠右將軍，張濟鎮東將軍。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, Li Jue personally became General of Chariots and Cavalry, Guo Si General of the Rear, Fan Chou General of the Right, and Zhang Ji General Who Guards the East.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Li Jue took the rank of general of chariots and cavalry, Guo Si became general of the rear, Fan Chou general of the right, and Zhang Ji general who guards the east.

sentences.zh: 濟出屯弘農。

translations.literal: Ji went out and encamped at Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ji marched out to garrison Hongnong.

sentences.zh: 甲申，司空淳于嘉為司徒，光祿大夫楊彪為司空，並錄尚書事。

translations.literal: On jiashen, Minister of Works Chunyu Jia was made Minister of Education, and Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Yang Biao was made Minister of Works, both supervising the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen Chunyu Jia moved from the ministry of works to the ministry of education, while Yang Biao became the minister of works; both were given secretariat oversight.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月，太尉皇甫嵩免。

translations.literal: In winter, the twelfth month, Grand Commandant Huangfu Song was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu Song was removed as grand commandant in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 光祿大夫周忠為太尉，參錄尚書事。

translations.literal: Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Zhou Zhong was made Grand Commandant and jointly supervised the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Zhong, a grandee of brilliant candidacy, became the grand commandant with shared secretariat authority.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月甲寅朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On jiayin, new moon of spring, the first month of the fourth year, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The new moon on jiayin in the first month brought a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On dingmao, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 三月，袁術殺楊州刺史陳溫，據淮南。

translations.literal: In the third month, Yuan Shu killed Inspector of Yang Chen Wen and occupied Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Yuan Shu murdered Chen Wen, inspector of Yang province, and seized Huainan.

sentences.zh: 長安宣平城門外屋自壞。

translations.literal: The buildings outside Chang’an’s Xueping Gate collapsed of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Structures outside the Xueping Gate in Chang’an fell in without apparent cause.

sentences.zh: 夏五月癸酉，無雲而雷。

translations.literal: On guiyou of summer, the fifth month, there was thunder though no clouds.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou in the fifth month thunder pealed from a clear sky.

sentences.zh: 六月，扶風大風，雨雹。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, in Fufeng there was great wind; hail fell as rain.
translations.idiomatic: The sixth month brought a violent windstorm and hail in Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 華山崩裂。

translations.literal: Mount Hua split and collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Hua suffered a landslide and fissures.

sentences.zh: 太尉周忠免，太僕朱儁為太尉，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Zhou Zhong was dismissed, and Grand Coachman Zhu Jun was made Grand Commandant and supervised the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Zhong stepped down as the grand commandant; Zhu Jun, the grand coachman, replaced him with secretariat duties.

sentences.zh: 下邳賊闕宣自稱天子。

translations.literal: The bandit Que Xuan of Xiapi styled himself Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Que Xuan, a rebel leader in Xiapi, declared himself emperor.

sentences.zh: 雨水。

translations.literal: Rain fell.
translations.idiomatic: Heavy rains continued.

sentences.zh: 遣侍御史裴茂訊詔獄，原輕繫。

translations.literal: He sent Attendant Censor Pei Mao to interrogate imperial-prison cases and release those lightly bound.
translations.idiomatic: Attendant censor Pei Mao was sent to review imperial jail cases and free minor offenders.

sentences.zh: 六月辛丑，天狗西北行。

translations.literal: On xinchou of the sixth month, the heavenly dog meteor moved northwest.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou a bright meteor, called the heavenly dog, streaked northwest across the sky.

sentences.zh: 九月甲午，試儒生四十餘人，上第賜位郎中，次太子舍人，下第者罷之。

translations.literal: On jiawu of the ninth month, more than forty scholars were examined; the top rank was granted gentleman consultant, the next householders for the heir apparent, and the lowest rank was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu over forty classicists were examined; high scorers became gentlemen consultant, the next tier heir’s householders, and failures were sent home.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『孔子歎「學之不講」，不講則所識日忘。

translations.literal: The edict said: “Confucius lamented that ‘scholarship is not cultivated’; if it is not cultivated, then what one knows is forgotten day by day.
translations.idiomatic: The edict ran: Confucius mourned that “learning is not pursued”; without pursuit, knowledge slips away daily.

sentences.zh: 今耆儒年踰六十，去離本土，營求糧資，不得專業。

translations.literal: Now venerable scholars are past sixty, have left their native soil, and in seeking grain and funds cannot devote themselves to their specialty.
translations.idiomatic: Elder scholars past sixty have left home to sit the exams yet must scramble for food and cannot study as they should.

sentences.zh: 結童入學，白首空歸，長委農野，永絕榮望，朕甚愍焉。

translations.literal: They entered school as children and return with white heads empty-handed, long abandoned in field and wild, their hopes of honor forever cut off—We are deeply pained.
translations.idiomatic: They began as boys and go home old men with nothing to show, cast back to the countryside and shut out from office—this fills Us with pity.

sentences.zh: 其依科罷者，聽為太子舍人。』

translations.literal: Those dismissed according to the regulations may be heard as householders for the heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: Those who fail the grading may nonetheless be appointed as householders to the heir apparent.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，太學行禮，車駕幸永福城門，臨觀其儀，賜博士以下各有差。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the Imperial Academy performed the rites; the imperial carriage visited Yongfu Gate and observed the ceremony, bestowing on the erudits and below each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month the university held its ceremony; the emperor watched from Yongfu Gate and rewarded the faculty in graded gifts.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，京師地震。

translations.literal: On xinchou, there was an earthquake in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou the capital region shook with an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 有星孛於天市。

translations.literal: There was a broom star in the Celestial Market Enclosure.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in the Celestial Market constellation.

sentences.zh: 司空楊彪免，太常趙溫為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Yang Biao was dismissed, and Minister of Ceremonies Zhao Wen was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Biao left the ministry of works; Zhao Wen, minister of ceremonies, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 公孫瓚殺大司馬劉虞。

translations.literal: Gongsun Zan killed Grand Marshal Liu Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Zan murdered Liu Yu, grand marshal.

sentences.zh: 興平元年春正月辛酉，大赦天下，改元興平。

translations.literal: In the first year of Xingping, spring, first month xinyou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven and changed the reign title to Xingping.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the first month of the first Xingping year a general amnesty was proclaimed and the era name changed to Xingping.

sentences.zh: 甲子，帝加元服。

translations.literal: On jiazi, the emperor added the capping-garment.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi the emperor underwent the capping ceremony and came of age.

sentences.zh: 二月壬午，追尊謚皇妣王氏為靈懷皇后，甲申，改葬於文昭陵。

translations.literal: On renwu of the second month, he posthumously honored his late mother née Wang with the title Empress Linghuai; on jiashen she was reburied at Wenzhao Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu the emperor’s mother, Lady Wang, was given the posthumous title Empress Linghuai; on jiashen her remains were moved to Wenzhao Mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 丁亥，帝耕於藉田。

translations.literal: On dinghai, the emperor plowed at the sacred field.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai the emperor performed the spring plowing rite at the ritual field.

sentences.zh: 三月，韓遂、馬騰與郭汜、樊稠戰於長平觀，遂、騰敗績，左中郎將劉範、前益州刺史种劭戰歿。

translations.literal: In the third month, Han Sui and Ma Teng fought Guo Si and Fan Chou at the Changping Lodge observation tower; Sui and Teng were routed; General of the Household for the Left Liu Fan and former Inspector of Yi Chong Shao died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Han Sui and Ma Teng clashed with Guo Si and Fan Chou at Changping Lodge and were beaten; Liu Fan and Chong Shao died in the fighting.

sentences.zh: 夏六月丙子，分涼州河西四郡為廱州。

translations.literal: In summer, sixth month bingzi, he divided the four commanderies west of the River in Liang province to form Yong province.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi in the sixth month the four Hexi commanderies were split off from Liang as a new Yong province.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，地震；

translations.literal: On dingchou, there was an earthquake;
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou the earth shook.

sentences.zh: 戊寅，又震。

translations.literal: On wuyin, again there was a quake.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin another tremor struck.

sentences.zh: 乙巳晦，日有食之，帝避正殿，寢兵，不聽事五日。

translations.literal: On yisi, last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse; the emperor avoided the main hall, rested weapons, and did not hear cases for five days.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse on the month’s last day, yisi, led the emperor to withdraw from court, stand down the guard, and suspend audiences for five days.

sentences.zh: 大蝗。

translations.literal: There were great locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed in devastating numbers.

sentences.zh: 秋七月壬子，太尉朱儁免。

translations.literal: On renzi of autumn, the seventh month, Grand Commandant Zhu Jun was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Zhu Jun was removed as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 戊午，太常楊彪為太尉，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: On wuwu, Minister of Ceremonies Yang Biao was made Grand Commandant and supervised the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu Yang Biao, the minister of ceremonies, became the grand commandant with secretariat authority.

sentences.zh: 三輔大旱，自四月至于是月。

translations.literal: The Three Adjuncts suffered great drought, from the fourth month until this month.
translations.idiomatic: From the fourth month through the seventh the metropolitan region baked under severe drought.

sentences.zh: 帝避正殿請雨，遣使者洗囚徒，原輕繫。

translations.literal: The emperor avoided the main hall to pray for rain, sent envoys to bathe prisoners, and released those lightly bound.
translations.idiomatic: He quit the main hall to seek rain, had prisoners ritually cleansed, and freed minor offenders.

sentences.zh: 是時穀一斛五十萬，豆麥一斛二十萬，人相食啖，白骨委積。

translations.literal: At this time grain was five hundred thousand cash per hu, beans and wheat two hundred thousand per hu; people ate one another; white bones piled up.
translations.idiomatic: Grain reached half a million cash a hu, beans and wheat two hundred thousand; cannibalism spread and bones littered the streets.

sentences.zh: 帝使侍御史侯汶出太倉米豆，為飢人作糜粥，經日而死者無降。

translations.literal: The emperor ordered Attendant Censor Hou Wen to issue rice and beans from the great granary and make gruel for the starving, yet after a full day the dead did not decrease.
translations.idiomatic: Hou Wen was told to draw grain from the imperial granary for gruel kitchens, but the daily death toll did not fall.

sentences.zh: 帝疑賦恤有虛，乃親於御坐前量試作糜，乃知非實，使侍中劉艾出讓有司。

translations.literal: The emperor suspected the relief distribution was false; he personally before the imperial seat measured and tested making gruel, then knew it was not true, and sent Palace Attendant Liu Ai out to reprove the responsible officials.
translations.idiomatic: Suspecting fraud in famine relief, the emperor had gruel prepared before his seat and proved the ration was short; he sent Liu Ai to rebuke the officials in charge.

sentences.zh: 於是尚書令以下皆詣省閣謝，奏收侯汶考實。

translations.literal: Thereupon the director of the secretariat and below all went to the secretariat gate to apologize, memorializing to seize Hou Wen and investigate the truth.
translations.idiomatic: The director of the secretariat downward went to apologize and asked that Hou Wen be arrested and questioned.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『未忍致汶於理，可杖五十。

translations.literal: The edict said, “I cannot bear to subject Wen to the law; he may be caned fifty strokes.”.
translations.idiomatic: The edict read, “I will not send Hou Wen to trial; give him fifty strokes of the rod.”.

sentences.zh: 』自是之後，多得全濟。

translations.literal: From this time afterward, many obtained full relief.
translations.idiomatic: After that, famine victims more often survived.

sentences.zh: 八月，馮翊羌叛，寇屬縣，郭汜、樊稠擊破之。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, the Fengyi Qiang rebelled, raiding dependent counties; Guo Si and Fan Chou attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Fengyi Qiang raiders struck outlying counties until Guo Si and Fan Chou drove them off.

sentences.zh: 九月，桑復生椹，人得以食。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, mulberries again bore fruit, and people were able to eat.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month mulberries flowered out of season, giving people something to eat.

sentences.zh: 司徒淳于嘉罷。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Chunyu Jia was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Chunyu Jia was dismissed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，長安市門自壞。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Chang’an’s market gates collapsed of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Chang’an’s market gates fell in on their own.

sentences.zh: 以衛尉趙溫為司徒，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: Commandant of the Guards Zhao Wen was made Minister of Education and supervised the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wen, commandant of the guards, became minister of education with secretariat duties.

sentences.zh: 十二月，分安定、扶風為新平郡。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, he divided Anding and Fufeng to form Xinping commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month territory was carved from Anding and Fufeng to create Xinping commandery.

sentences.zh: 是歲，楊州刺史劉繇與袁術將孫策戰於曲阿，繇軍敗績，孫策遂據江東。

translations.literal: That year, Inspector of Yang Liu Yao fought Yuan Shu’s general Sun Ce at Qu’e; Yao’s army was routed, and Sun Ce thereupon occupied the lands east of the Yangzi.
translations.idiomatic: That year Liu Yao met Sun Ce at Qu’e and lost; Sun Ce then took control of Wu east of the river.

sentences.zh: 太傅馬日磾薨於壽春。

translations.literal: Grand Tutor Ma Midi died at Shouchun.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Midi, grand tutor, died at Shouchun.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月癸丑，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, first month guichou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou in the first month of the second year a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 二月乙亥，李傕殺樊稠而與郭汜相攻。

translations.literal: On yihai of the second month, Li Jue killed Fan Chou and turned to attack Guo Si.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai Li Jue murdered Fan Chou and opened hostilities against the warlord Guo Si.

sentences.zh: 三月丙寅，李傕脅帝幸其營，焚宮室。

translations.literal: On bingyin of the third month, Li Jue coerced the emperor to visit his camp and burned the palace buildings.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin Li Jue forced the emperor into his encampment and torched the palaces.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲午，立貴人伏氏為皇后。

translations.literal: On jiawu of summer, the fourth month, the Worthy Lady née Fu was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the fourth month Worthy Lady Fu was elevated to empress.

sentences.zh: 丁酉，郭汜攻李傕，矢及御前。

translations.literal: On dingyou, Guo Si attacked Li Jue; arrows reached before the imperial presence.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou Guo Si’s assault on Li Jue brought missiles to the emperor’s very feet.

sentences.zh: 是日，李傕移帝幸北塢。

translations.literal: That day, Li Jue moved the emperor to visit the northern stockade.
translations.idiomatic: That same day Li Jue hustled the court into the northern walled camp.

sentences.zh: 大旱。

translations.literal: There was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: A punishing drought continued.

sentences.zh: 五月壬午，李傕自為大司馬。

translations.literal: On renwu of the fifth month, Li Jue personally became grand marshal.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu in the fifth month Li Jue took the title of grand marshal.

sentences.zh: 六月庚午，張濟自陜來和傕、汜。

translations.literal: On gengwu of the sixth month, Zhang Ji came from Shan to reconcile Li Jue and Guo Si.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu Zhang Ji arrived from Shan to mediate between Li Jue and Guo Si.

sentences.zh: 秋七月甲子，車駕東歸。

translations.literal: On jiazi of autumn, the seventh month, the imperial carriage set out eastward to return.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi in the seventh month the court began its march back east.

sentences.zh: 郭汜自為車騎將軍，楊定為後將軍，楊奉為興義將軍，董承為安集將軍，並侍送乘輿。

translations.literal: Guo Si personally became General of Chariots and Cavalry, Yang Ding General of the Rear, Yang Feng General Who Raises Righteousness, Dong Cheng General Who Pacifies and Gathers, all attending and escorting the imperial equipage.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Si took rank as general of chariots and cavalry while Yang Ding, Yang Feng, and Dong Cheng received generalcies and formed the escort for the emperor’s train.

sentences.zh: 張濟為票騎將軍，還屯陜。

translations.literal: Zhang Ji was made General of Agile Cavalry and returned to encamp at Shan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ji was named general of agile cavalry and went back to garrison Shan.

sentences.zh: 八月甲辰，幸新豐。

translations.literal: On jiachen of the eighth month, he visited Xinfeng.
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen the court reached Xinfeng.

sentences.zh: 冬十月戊戌，郭汜使其將伍習夜燒所幸學舍，逼脅乘輿。

translations.literal: On wuxu of winter, the tenth month, Guo Si had his general Wu Xi burn by night the school lodgings where the emperor had lodged, coercing the imperial equipage.
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu Guo Si sent Wu Xi to fire the school compound where the emperor was staying, pressing the court to move on.

sentences.zh: 楊定、楊奉與郭汜戰，破之。

translations.literal: Yang Ding and Yang Feng fought Guo Si and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ding and Yang Feng attacked Guo Si and broke his force.

sentences.zh: 壬寅，幸華陰，露次道南。

translations.literal: On renyin, he visited Huayin and bivouacked south of the road.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin the party halted south of the road at Huayin.

sentences.zh: 是夜，有赤氣貫紫宮。

translations.literal: That night, red vapors pierced the Purple Palace Enclosure.
translations.idiomatic: That night crimson light shot through the Purple Palace asterism.

sentences.zh: 張濟復反，與李傕、郭汜合。

translations.literal: Zhang Ji rebelled again and joined Li Jue and Guo Si.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ji turned coat again and allied with Li Jue and Guo Si.

sentences.zh: 十一月庚午，李傕、郭汜等追乘輿，戰於東澗，王師敗績，殺光祿勳鄧泉、衛尉士孫瑞、廷尉宣播、大長秋苗祀、步兵校尉魏桀、侍中朱展、射聲校尉沮儁。

translations.literal: On gengwu of the eleventh month, Li Jue, Guo Si, and others pursued the imperial equipage, fought at Eastern Ford, the royal army was routed, and they killed Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Deng Quan, Commandant of the Guards Shisun Rui, Commandant of Justice Xuan Bo, Grand Prolonger of Autumn Miao Si, Colonel of the Foot Soldiers Wei Jie, Palace Attendant Zhu Zhan, and Colonel of the Archers Who Let Their Sound Ring Ju Jun.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu Li Jue and Guo Si overtook the imperial column at Eastern Ford, shattered the escort, and slew Deng Quan, Shisun Rui, Xuan Bo, Miao Si, Wei Jie, Zhu Zhan, and Ju Jun.

sentences.zh: 壬申，幸曹陽，露次田中。

translations.literal: On renshen, he visited Caoyang and bivouacked in the fields.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen the court took refuge in the open fields at Caoyang.

sentences.zh: 楊奉、董承引白波帥胡才、李樂、韓暹及匈奴左賢王去卑，率師奉迎，與李傕等戰，破之。

translations.literal: Yang Feng and Dong Cheng brought the White Wave chiefs Hu Cai, Li Yue, Han Xian, and the Southern Xiongnu Worthy King of the Left Qubei, led troops to welcome and support, and fought Li Jue and others, defeating them.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Feng and Dong Cheng rallied Hu Cai, Li Yue, Han Xian, and the Xiongnu left worthy king Qubei, relieved the emperor, and turned back Li Jue’s pursuit.

sentences.zh: 十二月庚辰，車駕乃進。

translations.literal: On gengchen of the twelfth month, the imperial carriage then advanced.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen the column was able to move forward again.

sentences.zh: 李傕等復來追戰，王師大敗，殺略宮人，少府田芬、大司農張義等皆戰歿。

translations.literal: Li Jue and others again came in pursuit and battle; the royal army was greatly defeated; they killed and carried off palace women; Junior Prefect Tian Fen, Grand Minister of Agriculture Zhang Yi, and others all died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: Li Jue struck again, routing the escort, slaughtering and seizing ladies of the palace; Tian Fen, Zhang Yi, and others fell in the melee.

sentences.zh: 進幸陜，夜度河。

translations.literal: He advanced to visit Shan and crossed the river by night.
translations.idiomatic: The party pressed on to Shan and forded the river under cover of darkness.

sentences.zh: 乙亥，幸安邑。

translations.literal: On yihai, he visited Anyi.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai the emperor reached Anyi.

sentences.zh: 是歲，袁紹遣將麹義與公孫瓚戰於鮑丘，瓚軍大敗。

translations.literal: That year, Yuan Shao sent his general Qu Yi to fight Gongsun Zan at Baoqiu; Zan’s army was greatly defeated.
translations.idiomatic: That year the general Qu Yi, sent by Yuan Shao, crushed Gongsun Zan at Baoqiu.

sentences.zh: 秋七月甲子，車駕至洛陽，幸故中常侍趙忠宅。

translations.literal: On jiazi of autumn, the seventh month, the imperial carriage reached Luoyang and visited the former residence of Household Regular Attendant Zhao Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi in the seventh month the court entered Luoyang and lodged in the old mansion of Zhao Zhong.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，郊祀上帝，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On dingchou, he sacrificed at the suburban altar to High God and issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou he offered the suburban sacrifice to Heaven and proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 己卯，謁太廟。

translations.literal: On jimao, he visited the Grand Temple.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao he paid his respects at the dynastic temple.

sentences.zh: 八月辛丑，幸南宮楊安殿。

translations.literal: On xinchou of the eighth month, he visited the Yang’an Hall of the Southern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou the court took up the Yang’an Hall in the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，安國將軍張楊為大司馬，韓暹為大將軍，楊奉為車騎將軍。

translations.literal: On guimao, General Who Pacifies the State Zhang Yang was made grand marshal, Han Xian General-in-Chief, and Yang Feng General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao Zhang Yang became the grand marshal, Han Xian the general-in-chief, and Yang Feng the general of chariots and cavalry.

sentences.zh: 是時，宮室燒盡，百官披荊棘，依牆壁間。

translations.literal: At this time the palace halls were burned away; the hundred officials pushed through brambles and leaned between broken walls.
translations.idiomatic: The palaces were ash; ministers picked their way through thorns and huddled under ruined walls.

sentences.zh: 州郡各擁彊兵，而委輸不至，群僚飢乏，尚書郎以下自出采稆，或飢死牆壁間，或為兵士所殺。

translations.literal: The provinces and commanderies each held strong troops, yet tribute grain did not arrive; the host of officials hungered; secretariat gentlemen and below went out themselves to gather wild beans, some starving to death between the walls, some killed by soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: Regional warlords kept their armies home and sent no supplies; clerks starved; junior secretariat men foraged for wild grain and died in the ruins or at soldiers’ hands.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，鎮東將軍曹操自領司隸校尉，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: On xinhai, General Who Guards the East Cao Cao personally took Metropolitan Commandant and supervised the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai Cao Cao, general who guards the east, added metropolitan commandant and control of the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 曹操殺侍中臺崇、尚書馮碩等。

translations.literal: Cao Cao killed Palace Attendant Tai Chong, Director of the Secretariat Feng Shuo, and others.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao executed Tai Chong, Feng Shuo, and other courtiers.

sentences.zh: 封衛將軍董承為輔國將軍，伏完等十三人為列侯，贈沮儁為弘農太守。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed General of the Guards Dong Cheng as General Who Aids the State, thirteen men including Fu Wan as full marquises, and posthumously invested Ju Jun as governor of Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Cheng was promoted to general who aids the state, thirteen men including Fu Wan received marquisates, and Ju Jun was honored posthumously as governor of Hongnong.

sentences.zh: 庚申，遷都許。

translations.literal: On gengshen, he moved the capital to Xu.
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen the seat of government shifted to Xu.

sentences.zh: 己巳，幸曹操營。

translations.literal: On jisi, he visited Cao Cao’s camp.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi the emperor lodged in Cao Cao’s encampment.

sentences.zh: 九月，太尉楊彪、司空張喜罷。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, Grand Commandant Yang Biao and Minister of Works Zhang Xi were removed.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Yang Biao and Zhang Xi left the posts of grand commandant and minister of works.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月丙戌，曹操自為司空，行車騎將軍事，百官總己以聽。

translations.literal: On bingxu of winter, the eleventh month, Cao Cao personally became Minister of Works and acted the business of General of Chariots and Cavalry; the hundred officials tied up their seals to obey him.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu Cao Cao took the ministry of works and exercised the powers of general of chariots and cavalry; the bureaucracy answered only to him.

sentences.zh: 二年春，袁術自稱天子。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, Yuan Shu styled himself Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the second year at Xu Yuan Shu declared himself emperor.

sentences.zh: 三月，袁紹自為大將軍。

translations.literal: In the third month, Yuan Shao personally became General-in-Chief.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Yuan Shao assumed the title of general-in-chief.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，蝗。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts scourged the land in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 秋九月，漢水溢。

translations.literal: In autumn, the ninth month, the Han River overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: The Han River burst its banks in the ninth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲飢，江淮間民相食。

translations.literal: That year there was famine; between the Yangzi and Huai the people ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: Famine drove the people along the Yangzi and Huai to cannibalism.

sentences.zh: 袁術殺陳王寵。

translations.literal: Yuan Shu killed Prince Chong of Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shu murdered Liu Chong, prince of Chen.

sentences.zh: 孫策遣使奉貢。

translations.literal: Sun Ce sent envoys to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Ce dispatched envoys with tribute to the court.

sentences.zh: 三年夏四月，遣謁者裴茂率中郎將段煨討李傕，夷三族。

translations.literal: In the third year, summer, fourth month, he sent Herald Pei Mao leading General of the Household Duan Wei to campaign against Li Jue and exterminate his three clans.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of the third year Pei Mao and Duan Wei were sent to destroy Li Jue and extirpate his kin to the third degree.

sentences.zh: 呂布叛。

translations.literal: Lu Bu rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Bu rose in rebellion.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，盜殺大司馬張楊。

translations.literal: In winter, the eleventh month, bandits killed Grand Marshal Zhang Yang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month assassins slew Zhang Yang, grand marshal.

sentences.zh: 十二月癸酉，曹操擊呂布於徐州，斬之。

translations.literal: On guiyou of the twelfth month, Cao Cao attacked Lu Bu in Xu province and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou in the twelfth month Cao Cao defeated Lu Bu in Xu province and executed him.

sentences.zh: 四年春三月，袁紹攻公孫瓚於易京，獲之。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, third month, Yuan Shao attacked Gongsun Zan at Yijing and captured him.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month of the fourth year Yuan Shao took Yijing and captured Gongsun Zan.

sentences.zh: 衛將軍董承為車騎將軍。

translations.literal: General of the Guards Dong Cheng was made General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Cheng, general of the guards, was promoted to general of chariots and cavalry.

sentences.zh: 夏六月，袁術死。

translations.literal: In summer, the sixth month, Yuan Shu died.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shu died in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲，初置尚書左右僕射。

translations.literal: That year, for the first time he established left and right vice directors of the secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: That year the posts of left and right vice director of the secretariat were first created.

sentences.zh: 武陵女子死十四日復活。

translations.literal: A woman of Wuling died fourteen days and revived.
translations.idiomatic: A woman in Wuling who had been dead fourteen days reportedly returned to life.

sentences.zh: 是歲，孫策死，弟權襲其餘業。

translations.literal: That year, Sun Ce died; his younger brother Quan inherited his remaining enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: That year Sun Ce died and his brother Sun Quan took over his domain.

sentences.zh: 六年春二月丁卯朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On dingmao, new moon of spring, the second month of the sixth year, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The new moon on dingmao in the second month of the sixth year was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 七年夏五月庚戌，袁紹薨。

translations.literal: On gengxu of summer, the fifth month of the seventh year, Yuan Shao died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu in the fifth month of the seventh year Yuan Shao died.

sentences.zh: 于窴國獻馴象。

translations.literal: The state of Khotan presented tame elephants.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Khotan sent trained elephants as tribute.

sentences.zh: 是歲，越巂男子化為女子。

translations.literal: That year, a man of Yuexi commandery changed into a woman.
translations.idiomatic: That year a man in Yuexi was said to have transformed into a woman.

sentences.zh: 八年冬十月己巳，公卿初迎冬於北郊，總章始復備八佾舞。

translations.literal: On jisi of winter, the tenth month of the eighth year, the excellencies and ministers for the first time welcomed winter at the northern suburb, and the Hall of Grand Unity again fully prepared the eight-row dance.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi in the tenth month the court held the winter welcoming rite at the northern suburb and restored the eight-row dance at the Hall of Grand Unity.

sentences.zh: 初置司直官，督中都官。

translations.literal: For the first time he established the office of director upright, to supervise capital officials.
translations.idiomatic: The new post of director upright was created to oversee metropolitan officials.

sentences.zh: 九年秋八月戊寅，曹操大破袁尚，平冀州，自領冀州牧。

translations.literal: On wuyin of autumn, the eighth month of the ninth year, Cao Cao greatly defeated Yuan Shang, pacified Ji province, and personally took Shepherd of Ji.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin in the eighth month Cao Cao crushed Yuan Shang, subdued Ji province, and added the title shepherd of Ji.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，有星孛於東井。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, there was a broom star in Eastern Well.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in the Eastern Well constellation in the tenth month.

sentences.zh: 十二月，賜三公已下金、帛，各有差。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, he bestowed gold and silk on the three excellencies and below, each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month gold and silk were distributed down the official ladder by rank.

sentences.zh: 自是三年一賜，以為常制。

translations.literal: From this time every three years there was one such grant, taken as standing regulation.
translations.idiomatic: Henceforth such gifts were to be made every three years by rule.

sentences.zh: 十年春正月，曹操破袁譚於青州，斬之。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, spring, first month, Cao Cao defeated Yuan Tan in Qing province and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the tenth year Cao Cao destroyed Yuan Tan in Qing province and took his head.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，墨山賊張燕率眾降。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, the Mo Mountain bandit Zhang Yan led his host to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Zhang Yan of the Mo Mountain band surrendered with his followers.

sentences.zh: 秋九月，賜百官尤貧者金、帛，各有差。

translations.literal: In autumn, the ninth month, he bestowed gold and silk on the hundred officials who were especially poor, each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month the neediest officials received graded gifts of gold and silk.

sentences.zh: 十一年春正月，有星孛於北斗。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, spring, first month, there was a broom star in the Northern Dipper.
translations.idiomatic: A comet blazed in the Northern Dipper in the first month of the eleventh year.

sentences.zh: 三月，曹操破高幹於幷州，獲之。

translations.literal: In the third month, Cao Cao defeated Gao Gan in Bing province and captured him.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Cao Cao took Gao Gan in Bing province alive.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，武威太守張猛殺雍州刺史邯鄲商。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Wuwei Governor Zhang Meng killed Inspector of Yong Handan Shang.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Zhang Meng, governor of Wuwei, murdered Handan Shang, inspector of Yong.

sentences.zh: 是歲，立故琅邪王容子熙為琅邪王。

translations.literal: That year, he established the former king of Langye Rong’s son Xi as king of Langye.
translations.idiomatic: That year Liu Xi, son of the late Prince Rong of Langye, was installed as king of Langye.

sentences.zh: 齊、北海、阜陵、下邳、常山、甘陵、濟北、平原八國皆除。

translations.literal: The eight kingdoms of Qi, Beihai, Fuling, Xiapi, Changshan, Ganling, Jibei, and Pingyuan were all abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The eight vassal kingdoms of Qi, Beihai, Fuling, Xiapi, Changshan, Ganling, Jibei, and Pingyuan were abolished.

sentences.zh: 十二年秋八月，曹操大破烏桓於柳城，斬其蹋頓。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, autumn, eighth month, Cao Cao greatly defeated the Wuhuan at Liucheng and beheaded their Tadun.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month of the twelfth year Cao Cao shattered the Wuhuan at Liucheng and killed their chieftain Tadun.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛卯，有星孛於鶉尾。

translations.literal: On xinmao of winter, the tenth month, there was a broom star in the Quail’s Tail asterism.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao in the tenth month a comet appeared in the Quail’s Tail.

sentences.zh: 乙巳，黃巾賊殺濟南王赟。

translations.literal: On yisi, Yellow Turban bandits killed Prince Yun of Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi Yellow Turbans murdered Liu Yun, prince of Jinan.

sentences.zh: 十一月，遼東太守公孫康殺袁尚、袁熙。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Liaodong Governor Gongsun Kang killed Yuan Shang and Yuan Xi.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Gongsun Kang, governor of Liaodong, executed Yuan Shang and Yuan Xi.

sentences.zh: 十三年春正月，司徒趙溫免。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year, spring, first month, Minister of Education Zhao Wen was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the thirteenth year Zhao Wen was removed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 夏六月，罷三公官，置丞相、御史大夫。

translations.literal: In summer, the sixth month, he abolished the three excellencies’ offices and established chancellor and imperial counselor.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the three excellencies were abolished and replaced by a chancellor and an imperial counselor.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，曹操自為丞相。

translations.literal: On guisi, Cao Cao personally became chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: On guisi Cao Cao took the title of chancellor.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，曹操南征劉表。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Cao Cao campaigned south against Liu Biao.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Cao Cao marched south against Liu Biao.

sentences.zh: 八月丁未，光祿勳郗慮為御史大夫。

translations.literal: On dingwei of the eighth month, Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Xi Lu was made imperial counselor.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei Xi Lu, superintendent of the household for imperial proceedings, became imperial counselor.

sentences.zh: 壬子，曹操殺太中大夫孔融，夷其族。

translations.literal: On renzi, Cao Cao killed Grand Counselor Kong Rong and exterminated his clan.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi Cao Cao executed Kong Rong, the grand counselor, and wiped out his family.

sentences.zh: 是月，劉表卒，少子琮立，琮以荊州降操。

translations.literal: That month, Liu Biao died; his younger son Cong succeeded, and Cong surrendered Jing province to Cao.
translations.idiomatic: That same month Liu Biao died; his younger son Liu Cong succeeded and handed the Jing province to Cao Cao.

sentences.zh: 冬十月癸未朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On guiwei, new moon of winter, the tenth month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The tenth month opened on guiwei with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 曹操以舟師伐孫權，權將周瑜敗之於烏林、赤壁。

translations.literal: Cao Cao with a fleet campaigned against Sun Quan; Quan’s general Zhou Yu defeated him at Wulin and Chibi.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao attacked Sun Quan by water; Zhou Yu broke the northern fleet at Wulin and Chibi.

sentences.zh: 十四年冬十月，荊州地震。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, winter, tenth month, there was an earthquake in Jing province.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of the fourteenth year Jing province was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 十五年春二月乙巳朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On yisi, new moon of spring, the second month of the fifteenth year, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The second month of the fifteenth year opened on yisi with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 十七年夏五月癸未，誅衛尉馬騰，夷三族。

translations.literal: On guiwei of summer, the fifth month of the seventeenth year, Commandant of the Guards Ma Teng was executed and his three clans exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: On guiwei in the fifth month of the seventeenth year Ma Teng, commandant of the guards, was executed with extirpation to the third degree of kinship.

sentences.zh: 六月庚寅晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On gengyin, last day of the sixth month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse fell on the last day of the sixth month, gengyin.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，洧水、潁水溢。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the Wei and Ying rivers overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: The Wei and Ying rivers burst their banks in the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 螟。

translations.literal: There were crop borers.
translations.idiomatic: Borer insects damaged the crops.

sentences.zh: 八月，馬超破涼州，殺刺史韋康。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, Ma Chao broke Liang province and killed Inspector Wei Kang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Ma Chao overran Liang and slew Wei Kang, the inspector.

sentences.zh: 九月庚戌，立皇子熙為濟陰王，懿為山陽王，□為濟北王，敦為東海王。

translations.literal: On gengxu of the ninth month, the imperial son Xi was established as king of Jiyin, Yi as king of Shanyang, □ as king of Jibei, and Dun as king of Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu the emperor enfeoffed Liu Xi as king of Jiyin, Liu Yi as king of Shanyang, a third son as king of Jibei, and Liu Dun as king of Donghai; the received text omits one character of the third prince’s name.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月，星孛於五諸侯。

translations.literal: In winter, the twelfth month, a broom star appeared in the Five Feudal Lords asterism.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month a comet shone in the Five Feudal Lords constellation.

sentences.zh: 十八年春正月庚寅，復《禹貢》九州。

translations.literal: On gengyin of spring, the first month of the eighteenth year, he restored the nine provinces of the 《Tribute of Yu》.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the first month of the eighteenth year the administrative map was redrawn to match the nine provinces of the 《Tribute of Yu》.

sentences.zh: 十九年夏四月，旱。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year, summer, fourth month, there was drought.
translations.idiomatic: The fourth month of the nineteenth year brought drought.

sentences.zh: 五月，雨水。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, rain fell.
translations.idiomatic: Heavy rain fell in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 劉備破劉璋，據益州。

translations.literal: Liu Bei defeated Liu Zhang and occupied Yi province.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bei overthrew Liu Zhang and seized Yi province.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，曹操遣將夏侯淵討宋建於枹罕，獲之。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Cao Cao sent his general Xiahou Yuan to campaign against Song Jian at Fuhan and captured him.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Cao Cao dispatched Xiahou Yuan against Song Jian at Fuhan and took him captive.

sentences.zh: 十一月丁卯，曹操殺皇后伏氏，滅其族及二皇子。

translations.literal: On dingmao of the eleventh month, Cao Cao killed Empress née Fu and extinguished her clan and the two imperial sons.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao in the eleventh month Cao Cao executed Empress Fu, her entire clan, and her two young sons.

sentences.zh: 二十年春正月甲子，立貴人曹氏為皇后。

translations.literal: On jiazi of spring, the first month of the twentieth year, the Worthy Lady née Cao was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi in the first month of the twentieth year Worthy Lady Cao was elevated to empress.

sentences.zh: 賜天下男子爵，人一級，孝悌、力田二級。

translations.literal: He bestowed noble ranks on the men of the empire, one grade each; filial and fraternal conduct and exert-fielders two grades.
translations.idiomatic: Every man in the realm received one step in rank; model sons and model farmers received two.

sentences.zh: 賜諸王侯公卿以下穀，各有差。

translations.literal: He bestowed grain on the kings, marquises, excellencies, and ministers of state and below, each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: Grain was distributed to princes and officials down the ladder in graded amounts.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，曹操破漢中，張魯降。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Cao Cao broke Hanzhong and Zhang Lu surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Cao Cao conquered Hanzhong and Zhang Lu submitted.

sentences.zh: 二十一年夏四月甲午，曹操自進號魏王。

translations.literal: On jiawu of summer, the fourth month of the twenty-first year, Cao Cao personally advanced his title to king of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the fourth month of the twenty-first year Cao Cao took the title king of Wei.

sentences.zh: 五月己亥朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On jihai, new moon of the fifth month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The fifth month opened on jihai with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，匈奴南單于來朝。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the Southern Shanyu of the Xiongnu came to court.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month the Southern Xiongnu Shanyu paid a formal visit to the court.

sentences.zh: 是歲，曹操殺琅邪王熙，國除。

translations.literal: That year, Cao Cao killed Prince Xi of Langye and abolished the kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: That year Cao Cao executed Liu Xi, king of Langye, and abolished the kingdom.

sentences.zh: 二十二年夏六月，丞相軍師華歆為御史大夫。

translations.literal: In the twenty-second year, summer, sixth month, Chancellor’s Army Counselor Hua Xin was made imperial counselor.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month of the twenty-second year Hua Xin, the chancellor’s army counselor, became imperial counselor.

sentences.zh: 冬，有星孛於東北。

translations.literal: In winter, there was a broom star in the northeast.
translations.idiomatic: A comet blazed in the northeastern sky that winter.

sentences.zh: 是歲大疫。

translations.literal: That year there was a great plague.
translations.idiomatic: A terrible epidemic swept the land that year.

sentences.zh: 二十三年春正月甲子，少府耿紀、丞相司直韋晃起兵誅曹操，不克，夷三族。

translations.literal: On jiazi of spring, the first month of the twenty-third year, Junior Prefect Geng Ji and Chancellor’s Director Upright Wei Huang raised troops to execute Cao Cao; they did not overcome him; their three clans were exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi Geng Ji and Wei Huang attempted a coup against Cao Cao and failed; both men’s kin to the third degree were slaughtered.

sentences.zh: 三月，有星孛於東方。

translations.literal: In the third month, there was a broom star in the east.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in the eastern sky in the third month.

sentences.zh: 二十四年春二月壬子晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On renzi, last day of spring, the second month of the twenty-fourth year, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The second month closed on renzi with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，劉備取漢中。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, Liu Bei took Hanzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Bei seized Hanzhong in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚子，劉備自稱漢中王。

translations.literal: On gengzi of autumn, the seventh month, Liu Bei styled himself king of Hanzhong.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the seventh month Liu Bei proclaimed himself king of Hanzhong.

sentences.zh: 八月，漢水溢。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, the Han River overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: The Han River flooded in the eighth month.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，孫權取荊州。

translations.literal: In winter, the eleventh month, Sun Quan took Jing province.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Sun Quan conquered Jing province.

sentences.zh: 冬十月乙卯，皇帝遜位，魏王丕稱天子。

translations.literal: On yimao of winter, the tenth month, the emperor yielded the throne, and the king of Wei, Pi, assumed the title Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On yimao in the tenth month the emperor abdicated and Cao Pi, king of Wei, became emperor.

sentences.zh: 奉帝為山陽公，邑一萬戶，位在諸侯王上，奏事不稱臣，受詔不拜，以天子車服郊祀天地，宗廟、祖、臘皆如漢制，都山陽之濁鹿城。

translations.literal: He honored the emperor as duke of Shanyang, with a fief of ten thousand households, rank above the kings of the feudal lords, memorializing affairs without styling himself subject, receiving edicts without bowing, using the Son of Heaven’s carriages and robes to sacrifice to Heaven and Earth at the suburban altars, with ancestral temple, offering, and year-end rites all as under Han institutions, capital at Zhuolu city in Shanyang.
translations.idiomatic: The former emperor was enfeoffed as duke of Shanyang with ten thousand households, precedence over other princes, privilege to address the throne without calling himself a subject, to receive edicts without bowing, to worship Heaven and Earth with imperial regalia, and to keep Han ritual at his court in Zhuolu.

sentences.zh: 四皇子封王者，皆降為列侯。

translations.literal: The four imperial sons who had been enfeoffed as kings were all reduced to full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: The four imperial sons who held kingships were demoted to marquises.

sentences.zh: 明年，劉備稱帝於蜀，孫權亦自王於吳，於是天下遂三分矣。

translations.literal: The next year, Liu Bei declared himself emperor in Shu, and Sun Quan likewise declared himself king in Wu; thereupon all under Heaven was split in three.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Liu Bei took the imperial title in Shu and Sun Quan declared himself king in Wu, and the realm split three ways.

sentences.zh: 魏青龍二年三月庚寅，山陽公薨。

translations.literal: On gengyin of the third month in the second year of Qinglong of Wei, the duke of Shanyang died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the third month of Wei’s second Qinglong year the duke of Shanyang died.

sentences.zh: 自遜位至薨，十有四年，年五十四，謚孝獻皇帝。

translations.literal: From yielding the throne until his death was fourteen years; he was fifty-four; his posthumous title was Emperor Xiaoxian.
translations.idiomatic: Fourteen years passed from his abdication to his death at the age of fifty-four; he received the posthumous title Emperor Xian.

sentences.zh: 八月壬申，以漢天子禮儀葬於禪陵，置園邑令丞。

translations.literal: On renshen of the eighth month, he was buried at Shan Mausoleum with the rites of a Han Son of Heaven, establishing park and fief magistrate and assistant.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen in the eighth month he was interred at Shan Mausoleum with full Han imperial honors and officials to tend the tomb.

sentences.zh: 論曰：傳稱鼎之為器，雖小而重，故神之所寶，不可奪移。

translations.literal: The discussion says: The tradition states that as a vessel the tripod, though small, is heavy; therefore it is what the spirits treasure and cannot be wrested away.
translations.idiomatic: The historian comments: the classics compare the throne to a bronze tripod—small yet weighty, a thing the spirits protect and none may seize by force.

sentences.zh: 至令負而趨者，此亦窮運之歸乎！

translations.literal: As for him who to this day bears it on his back yet hastens—is this not likewise the end of exhausted fate!
translations.idiomatic: Yet here was a man who bore that burden on his back while being driven onward—was this not fate run to its bitter end!

sentences.zh: 天厭漢德久矣，山陽其何誅焉！

translations.literal: Heaven has long wearied of Han virtue—what blame attaches to the duke of Shanyang!
translations.idiomatic: Heaven had turned against Han’s mandate long before; the duke of Shanyang hardly deserved reproach!

sentences.zh: 贊曰：獻生不辰，身播國屯。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Xian was born untimely; his person was cast about while the state met hardship.
translations.idiomatic: The summation reads: Emperor Xian was born under an ill star, his person tossed from throne to exile while the dynasty stumbled.

sentences.zh: 終我四百，永作虞賓。

translations.literal: He ended our four hundred years, forever to be a guest of Shun.
translations.idiomatic: Thus ended the Han’s four centuries, its last sovereign standing as a guest king like the heirs of Shun before the Xia.
